title: About
text:
These pages contain the code for the analysis and generation of figures discussed in our recent manuscript link (TBC)


Rotem Aharon1,2, Alicia Oshlack1,2,3




Computational Biology Program, Peter MacCallum Cancer Centre, Parkville, VIC, Australia
Sir Peter MacCallum Department of Oncology, University of Melbourne, Parkville, VIC, Australia
School of Mathematics and Statistics, University of Melbourne, Parkville, VIC, Australia




Previously, RNA sequencing required Reverse Transcription (RT) and PCR amplification. PCR amplification is known to induce biases associated with expression levels, transcript lengths and GC content. Biases introduced by RT have been described more recently, and their quantification has been more elusive. Oxford Nanopore Technology (ONT) enables the direct sequencing of RNA without RT, and also the sequencing of cDNA without PCR amplification. This allows us to gain further insights into how different aspects of RNA preparation bias the data, with a new focus on isoforms structure and abundance.
Using seven cell line data sets from SG-NEx, we compare nanopore RNA sequencing of direct RNA (dRNA), direct cDNA (dcDNA) and PCR amplified cDNA (PCRcDNA). Across all the cell lines we identify hundreds of Differentially Expressed (DE) genes and isoforms, indicating a significant change in observed expression between the protocols. Furthermore, we identify Differential Transcript Usage (DTU) between protocols in hundreds of genes across cell lines, indicating significant changes in the measured isoform proportions.
Analysis of DE genes and isoforms in the context of expression levels, sequence length and GC content shows that, as expected, the largest number of DE isoforms is between the PCR samples and non-PCR samples. DE isoforms display known amplification bias with respect to expression level, sequence length and GC content. Interestingly we also observe decreased counts after PCR associated with higher levels of the m6A methylation mark on the native RNA.
We also analyse structural differences between isoforms that switch in dominance between direct RNA and PCR cDNA samples, and find that while some switching events relate to simple fragmentation/truncation of the RNA, most involve more complex structural differences including novel junctions, such as exon truncation, exon skipping and intron retention. When compared to differences found between background transcripts selected from non DTU genes, inserted introns (exitrons) are significantly more common in the isoforms after RT and amplification. Last, we find that visualisation of raw coverage data is a crucial step in the qualitative validation of experimental findings and should be included to validate analysis results.



For attribution, please cite our work (DOI: TBC).
Long read sequencing reveals changes in measured isoform structure and abundance due to RNA processing
Rotem Aharon, Alicia Oshlack

title: 1 Data overview PacBio samples included  (Figure 1)
text: The data analysed here is publicly available from the SG-Nex database (Ying Chen et al. 2025). The SG-NEx data was accessed in June 2025 registry.opendata.aws/sg-nex-data. We started by downloading 107 .bam files (samples), which include read alignment to the human genome (hg38) for each of the replicates in each of the cell lines. ‘bambu’ v3.8.3 with NDR=0.1 was used to identify and quantify the genes and transcripts in all of the samples together. This resulted in a SummarisedExperiment R object, which includes transcript counts, and full length read counts as separate assays. Gene counts were obtained by using bambu::transcriptToGeneExpression, which effectively adds the transcript counts for each gene. For each sample, library size was obtained by adding up all transcript counts. Samples with very low or very high library size <400,000 or >40,000,000 were excluded, as well as those that had full length to all transcript ratio lower than 0.2. With the remaining 79 samples, MDS dimensional reduction plots were generated using edgeR::plotMDS for each of the assays independently, with top=2000, to account for the fact transcript counts are not independent of each other.
crumbs:
1 Data overview PacBio samples included
(Figure 1)

title: 1 Data overview PacBio samples included  (Figure 1)
section: 1.1 Library size based on Transcript counts
text:
1.1 Library size based on Transcript counts


Code
libsize_low_lim = 4e+05

libsize_high_lim = 4e+07
all_colData <- data.frame(colData(analysis_se_no_ev))

# plot all library sizes in a boxplot
f1a <- all_colData %>%
    ggplot(aes(cellType, log2(counts_libsize), color = protocol)) + geom_boxplot() +
    geom_jitter(shape = 16, position = position_dodge(width = 0.75)) + geom_hline(yintercept = log2(libsize_low_lim)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = log2(libsize_high_lim)) + labs(x = "", y = "log2[library size (all transcript counts)") +
    theme(legend.position = "none")

f1a









Adjust plot limits for better representation of variability between samples


Code
f1a <- f1a + coord_cartesian(ylim = c(14, 26.5))
f1a

title: 1 Data overview PacBio samples included  (Figure 1)
section: 1.2 Full length to all count library size ratios
text:
1.2 Full length to all count library size ratios


Code
cell_line_colours <- RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(9, "Oranges")
# hex <- hue_pal()(6)
named_cell_line_colours <- list(A549 = cell_line_colours[3], H9 = cell_line_colours[4],
    HEYA8 = cell_line_colours[5], HepG2 = cell_line_colours[6], Hct116 = cell_line_colours[7],
    K562 = cell_line_colours[8], MCF7 = cell_line_colours[9])



p2 <- all_colData %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = log2(counts_libsize), y = fullLengthCounts_libsize/counts_libsize,
        color = cellType, shape = protocol)) + scale_shape_manual(values = c(16,
    17, 4, 15)) + geom_point(, size = 3, stroke = 1) + geom_vline(xintercept = log2(libsize_low_lim)) +
    annotate(geom = "text", x = log2(libsize_low_lim - 50000), y = 0.55, label = "Library size lower limit",
        angle = "90") + geom_hline(yintercept = 0.2) + annotate(geom = "text", x = 23,
    y = 0.22, label = "Full-length / all transcripts = 0.2", angle = "0") + theme(legend.position = "none") +
    scale_color_manual(values = named_cell_line_colours) + labs(x = "Library size (log2, all transcript counts)",
    y = "Library size ratio \n (Full length transcripts)/(all transcripts)")

p2









Adjust plot limits for better representation of variability between samples


Code
f1b <- p2 + coord_cartesian(xlim = c(16, 25))
f1b

title: 1 Data overview PacBio samples included  (Figure 1)
text:
2.1 Figure 1: Data overview


Code
numPlots <- ggarrange(f1a, f1b, labels = "auto", common.legend = TRUE, legend = "left",
    ncol = 2, nrow = 1)


pdf("allDataOverview_with_pacbio.pdf", width = 12, height = 12)
dataOverview <- ggarrange(numPlots, MDSplots, nrow = 2, heights = c(1.2, 3))
dataOverview
invisible(dev.off())


png("allDataOverview_with_pacbio.png", width = 30, height = 30, units = "cm", bg = "white",
    res = 300)
dataOverview <- ggarrange(numPlots, MDSplots, nrow = 2, heights = c(1.2, 3))

dataOverview
invisible(dev.off())











Figure 1: a) Library size (number of mapped reads) in each sample, across preparation protocols and cell lines. The samples between the horizontal lines were retained for analysis. b) Fraction of full length transcript reads as a function of log2(library size). MDS plots of gene counts (c,d), total transcript counts (e,f) and full length transcript counts (g,h). Samples separate based on cell line (colour) but also on preparation protocol (shape) especially at the isoform level.












Note



The total number of samples quantified by bambu is 105.
After filtering out samples based on library size (only include 4^{5} < transcripts library size < 4^{7}), there are 77 samples included in the MDS plots.

title: 1 Data overview PacBio samples included  (Figure 1)
text:
3.1 Samples list and exlusion criteria

3.1.1 Create table


Code
downloaded_samples_table <- data.frame(colData(analysis_se)) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(sample_name = name, library_size = counts_libsize, cell_line = cellType,
        grouped_protocol = protocol) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(protocol = raw_protocol) %>%
    select(-c(raw_protocol, cellType, group, gene_counts_libsize))



downloaded_samples_table$in_1a_b <- downloaded_samples_table$cell_line != "MCF7-EV"
downloaded_samples_table$in_1a_b <- ifelse(downloaded_samples_table$in_1a_b, "Yes",
    "No")



downloaded_samples_table$exluded_from_1c_h <- downloaded_samples_table$cell_line ==
    "MCF7-EV" | downloaded_samples_table$counts_libsize < libsize_low_lim | downloaded_samples_table$counts_libsize >
    libsize_high_lim | downloaded_samples_table$fullLengthCounts_libsize/downloaded_samples_table$counts_libsize <
    0.2
downloaded_samples_table$in_1c_h <- ifelse(downloaded_samples_table$exluded_from_1c_h,
    "No", "Yes")




Code
ONTonly_samples <- "../../data/bambu_output/ont_only_counts/se_bambu_result.rds"

ont_all_quantification_results <- readRDS(ONTonly_samples)
ont_all_quantification_results <- add_dimReductions(ont_all_quantification_results,
    calc_mds = FALSE)


[1] "counts"
[1] "fullLengthCounts"
[1] "gene_counts"


Code
ont_all_quantified_samples <- colData(ont_all_quantification_results)

missinig_samples_names <- setdiff(ont_all_quantified_samples$name, downloaded_samples_table$name)
missinig_samples_names


[1] "SGNex_A549_cDNAStranded_replicate3_run3"




Code
# Add values manually for now
downloaded_samples_table[missinig_samples_names, ] <- c("SGNex_A549_cDNAStranded_replicate3_run3",
    "cDNAStranded", NA, NA, "SGNex_A549_cDNAStranded_replicate3_run3", NA, "A549",
    "PCRcDNA", "No", TRUE, "No")


ont_all_quantified_samples <- ont_all_quantified_samples[, c("name", "counts_libsize")]
colnames(ont_all_quantified_samples) <- c("name", "ONTrun_counts_libsize")


downloaded_samples_table <- downloaded_samples_table %>%
    full_join(data.frame(ont_all_quantified_samples), by = join_by("name"))


downloaded_samples_table$in_sup1a_b <- downloaded_samples_table$grouped_protocol !=
    "PacBio-SMRTcell" & downloaded_samples_table$cell_line != "MCF7-EV"

downloaded_samples_table$in_sup1a_b <- ifelse(downloaded_samples_table$in_sup1a_b,
    "Yes", "No")

selected_samples_ont_only_se_path <- "../../data/analysis_output/ont_only_counts/selected_samples.rds"

ont_selected_samples_se <- readRDS(selected_samples_ont_only_se_path)
downloaded_samples_table$in_sup1c_h <- downloaded_samples_table$name %in% colData(ont_selected_samples_se)$name




downloaded_samples_table$exluded_from_DTU <- !downloaded_samples_table$in_sup1c_h |
    all_colData$protocol == "PacBio-SMRTcell" | all_colData$protocol == "directcDNA"


downloaded_samples_table$in_sup1c_h <- ifelse(downloaded_samples_table$in_sup1c_h,
    "Yes", "No")
downloaded_samples_table$in_DE_fig2 <- downloaded_samples_table$in_sup1c_h
downloaded_samples_table$in_DTU_fig3 <- ifelse(downloaded_samples_table$exluded_from_DTU,
    "No", "Yes")




Code
downloaded_samples_table <- downloaded_samples_table %>%
    select(-c(name, exluded_from_1c_h, exluded_from_DTU, counts_libsize, fullLengthCounts_libsize)) %>%
    relocate(c(protocol, grouped_protocol), .before = cell_line)  # %>% relocate(c(protocol,grouped_protocol), .before = cell_line)


downloaded_samples_table <- downloaded_samples_table[order(downloaded_samples_table$cell_line,
    downloaded_samples_table$protocol), ]

# Count number of samples included in each column
yes_counts <- colSums(downloaded_samples_table == "Yes", na.rm = TRUE)

# Append to the bottom of the data frame
downloaded_samples_table <- rbind(downloaded_samples_table, yes_counts)

# Give the new row a clear row name
rownames(downloaded_samples_table)[nrow(downloaded_samples_table)] <- "Total"




Code
knitr::kable(x = downloaded_samples_table, caption = paste0("Sample inclusion table"),
    row.names = FALSE)

section: 3.2 Auto format table to pdf (using latex)
text:
3.2 Auto format table to pdf (using latex)


Code
latex_table <- kable(downloaded_samples_table, row.names = FALSE, format = "latex",
    booktabs = TRUE, longtable = TRUE, escape = TRUE, caption = "Sample inclusion table",
    align = rep("c", ncol(downloaded_samples_table))) %>%
    kable_styling(latex_options = c("striped", "hold_position", "repeat_header"),
        font_size = 6, full_width = FALSE, position = "center")

# Use landscape geometry instead of kableExtra's landscape()
latex_doc <- paste0("\\documentclass{article}\n", "\\usepackage[utf8]{inputenc}\n",
    "\\usepackage{booktabs}\n", "\\usepackage{longtable}\n", "\\usepackage{array}\n",
    "\\usepackage{colortbl}\n", "\\usepackage{xcolor}\n", "\\usepackage[landscape, margin=0.5in]{geometry}\n",
    "\\begin{document}\n", latex_table, "\n\\end{document}")

# Write LaTeX file
writeLines(latex_doc, "table1.tex")

# Compile to PDF (requires tinytex or LaTeX installation)
tinytex::pdflatex("table1.tex")


[1] "table1.pdf"





Table 1: Sample inclusion table

1 Data overview PacBio samples included
(Figure 1)

title: 2 Data overview ONT samples only  (Supp. Figure 1)
text:
SG-Nex data analysed here is publicly available from the SG-Nex database (Ying Chen et al. 2025). It was accessed in June 2025 at registry.opendata.aws/sg-nex-data. ONT samples only quantification started from 100 .bam files (ONT samples only), which include read alignment to the human genome (hg38) for each of the replicates in each of the cell lines. Then bambu (‘bambu’ v3.8.3 with NDR=0.1) was used to identify and quantify the genes and transcripts in all of the samples together.
This resulted in a SummarisedExperiment R object, from which this analysis page starts. It includes transcript counts and full length read counts as separate assays. Gene counts were obtained from it by using bambu::transcriptToGeneExpression() function, which effectively adds up all transcript counts for each gene.

title: 2 Data overview ONT samples only  (Supp. Figure 1)
text:
1.1 Library size (all transcript counts)


Code
libsize_low_lim = 4e+05

libsize_high_lim = 4e+07
all_colData <- data.frame(colData(analysis_se))

# plot all library sizes in a boxplot
f1a <- all_colData %>%
    ggplot(aes(cellType, log2(counts_libsize), color = protocol)) + geom_boxplot() +
    geom_jitter(shape = 16, position = position_dodge(width = 0.75)) + geom_hline(yintercept = log2(libsize_low_lim)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = log2(libsize_high_lim)) + labs(x = "", y = "log2[library size (all transcript counts)") +
    theme(legend.position = "none")

f1a









Adjust plot limits for better representation of variability between samples


Code
f1a <- f1a + coord_cartesian(ylim = c(14, 26.5))
f1a

cellLine_colors <- RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(9, "Oranges")

    HepG2 = cellLine_colors[6], Hct116 = cellLine_colors[7], K562 = cellLine_colors[8],
    MCF7 = cellLine_colors[9])

    ggplot(aes(x = log2(counts_libsize), y = fullLengthCounts_libsize/counts_libsize,
        color = cellType, shape = protocol)) + scale_shape_manual(values = c(16,
    17, 4, 15)) + geom_point(, size = 3, stroke = 1) + geom_vline(xintercept = log2(libsize_low_lim)) +
    annotate(geom = "text", x = log2(libsize_low_lim - 50000), y = 0.55, label = "Library size lower limit",
        angle = "90") + geom_hline(yintercept = 0.2) + annotate(geom = "text", x = 23,
    y = 0.22, label = "Full-length / all transcripts = 0.2", angle = "0") + theme(legend.position = "none") +

    scale_color_manual(values = named_cell_line_colours) + #theme(legend.position = 'none')+ scale_color_manual(values= named_cell_line_colours) + =

    scale_color_manual(values = named_cell_line_colours) + #theme(legend.position = 'none')+ scale_color_manual(values= named_cell_line_colours) + =
    scale_color_manual(values = named_cell_line_colours) + #theme(legend.position = 'none')+ scale_color_manual(values= named_cell_line_colours) + 'none')+
    scale_color_manual(values = named_cell_line_colours) + #theme(legend.position = 'none')+ scale_color_manual(values= named_cell_line_colours) + scale_color_manual(values=
    scale_color_manual(values = named_cell_line_colours) + #theme(legend.position = 'none')+ scale_color_manual(values= named_cell_line_colours) + named_cell_line_colours)
    scale_color_manual(values = named_cell_line_colours) + #theme(legend.position = 'none')+ scale_color_manual(values= named_cell_line_colours) + +
labs(x = "Library size (log2, all transcript counts)", y = "Library size ratio \n (Full-length transcripts only)/(all transcripts)")

section: 4.1 Explore possible impact of library size and stransded cDNA join with cDNA
text:
4.1 Explore possible impact of library size and stransded cDNA join with cDNA
Below we keep PCRcDNA and stranded PCR cDNA samples seperated, to view variation between them.

4.1.1 Library size based on all transcript counts


Code
# plot all library sizes in a boxplot
f1a <- all_colData %>%
    ggplot(aes(cellType, log2(counts_libsize), color = raw_protocol)) + geom_boxplot() +
    geom_jitter(shape = 16, position = position_dodge(width = 0.75)) + geom_hline(yintercept = log2(libsize_low_lim)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = log2(libsize_high_lim)) + labs(x = "", y = "log2[library size (all transcript counts)") +
    theme(legend.position = "left")

f1a









Adjust plot limits for better representation of variability between samples


Code
f1a <- f1a + coord_cartesian(ylim = c(14, 26.5))
f1a











4.1.2 Plot ratio between full length to all transcript count library size


Code
p2 <- all_colData %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = log2(counts_libsize), y = fullLengthCounts_libsize/counts_libsize,
        color = cellType, shape = raw_protocol)) + scale_shape_manual(values = c(16,
    1, 17, 4, 15)) + geom_point(, size = 3, stroke = 1) + geom_vline(xintercept = log2(libsize_low_lim)) +
    annotate(geom = "text", x = log2(libsize_low_lim - 50000), y = 0.55, label = "Library size lower limit",
        angle = "90") + geom_hline(yintercept = 0.2) + annotate(geom = "text", x = 23,
    y = 0.22, label = "Full-length / all transcripts = 0.2", angle = "0") + theme(legend.position = "bottom") +
    scale_color_manual(values = named_cell_line_colours) + labs(x = "Library size (log2, all transcript counts)",
    y = "Library size ratio \n (Full length transcripts)/(all transcripts)")

p2









Adjust plot limits for better representation of variability between samples


Code
f1b_highlight_stranded <- p2 + coord_cartesian(xlim = c(16, 25))
f1b_highlight_stranded











4.1.3 MDS plots of selected samples


Code
selected_samples_all_colData <- data.frame(colData(selected_samples))


p3 <- selected_samples_all_colData %>%
    ggplot(aes(gene_counts_mds_1, gene_counts_mds_2)) %>%
    mds_aes(shapes = c(16, 1, 17, 4, 15), shape_var = "raw_protocol", size_var = "gene_counts_libsize") +
    labs(x = "MDS_1", y = "MDS_2", title = "Gene counts") + guides(shape = guide_legend(title = "protocol",
    title.position = "top", nrow = 2), color = guide_legend(title = "cell line",
    title.position = "top", nrow = 2), size = guide_legend(title = "library size",
    title.position = "top", nrow = 2))

p4 <- selected_samples_all_colData %>%
    ggplot(aes(gene_counts_mds_3, gene_counts_mds_4)) %>%
    mds_aes(shapes = c(16, 1, 17, 4, 15), shape_var = "raw_protocol", size_var = "gene_counts_libsize") +
    labs(x = "MDS_3", y = "MDS_4", title = "Gene counts") + guides(shape = guide_legend(title = "protocol",
    title.position = "top", nrow = 2), color = guide_legend(title = "cell line",
    title.position = "top", nrow = 2), size = guide_legend(title = "library size",
    title.position = "top", nrow = 2))

p5 <- selected_samples_all_colData %>%
    ggplot(aes(counts_mds_1, counts_mds_2)) %>%
    mds_aes(shapes = c(16, 1, 17, 4, 15), shape_var = "raw_protocol", size_var = "counts_libsize") +
    labs(x = "MDS_1", y = "MDS_2", title = "Isoform counts (all TX's)") + guides(shape = guide_legend(title = "protocol",
    title.position = "top", nrow = 2), color = guide_legend(title = "cell line",
    title.position = "top", nrow = 2), size = guide_legend(title = "library size",
    title.position = "top", nrow = 2))

p6 <- selected_samples_all_colData %>%
    ggplot(aes(counts_mds_3, counts_mds_4)) %>%
    mds_aes(shapes = c(16, 1, 17, 4, 15), shape_var = "raw_protocol", size_var = "counts_libsize") +
    labs(x = "MDS_3", y = "MDS_4", title = "Isoform counts (all TX's)") + guides(shape = guide_legend(title = "protocol",
    title.position = "top", nrow = 2), color = guide_legend(title = "cell line",
    title.position = "top", nrow = 2), size = guide_legend(title = "library size",

crumbs:

title: 3 Differential Expression between protocols  (Figure 2)
text: Differential Expression analysis was performed separately for each cell line, with a design matrix constructed based on the sequencing protocol. For each of the seven cell lines, three count matrices were obtained from the relevant samples in the SummarisedExperiment which holds all the selected samples outlined above. For each of the count matrices (gene counts, transcript counts, and transcript counts obtained based on full length read only), the raw reads were filtered to remove low expression, with min_count = 20 and min_total_count = 50 for genes counts, and min_count = 10, min_total_count = 20 for transcript counts. After filtering, voomWithQualityWeights was utilised to adjust for varying sample quality, and a linear model was fitted with lmFit. Next, limma-voom::decideTest was used with p.value=0.05, and lf = 1.0 or 0.5 for genes and transcripts respectively, and topTables were obtained for each of the comparisons in each of the cell lines.

title: 3 Differential Expression between protocols  (Figure 2)
text:
1.1 Differential Gene Expression

1.1.1 Figure 2a


Code
library(scales)
hex <- hue_pal()(6)
colors_allocation_list = list(directRNA = hex[5], directcDNA = hex[3], PCRcDNA = hex[1],
    dcDNAvsPCRcDNA = hex[2], dRNAvsdcDNA = hex[4], dRNAvsPCRcDNA = hex[6])

contrasts_order = c("dRNAvsPCRcDNA", "dcDNAvsPCRcDNA", "dRNAvsdcDNA")

case_prefix <- "gene_counts"


longer_de_labels <- new_de_labels_longer %>%
    mutate(contrast = name) %>%
    dplyr::filter(assay == case_prefix)
de_genes_fraction <- longer_de_labels %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(value)) %>%
    dplyr::select(c(contrast, cell_line, value)) %>%
    group_by(cell_line, contrast) %>%
    mutate(number_tested = n()) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    group_by(cell_line, contrast, value) %>%
    mutate(n = n()) %>%
    dplyr::filter(value != 0) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = cell_line, y = n/number_tested, fill = factor(contrast, levels = contrasts_order))) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge()) + scale_fill_manual(values = colors_allocation_list) +
    labs(y = "Fraction of DE genes", x = "", fill = "")
de_genes_fraction

Mean Expression presents values obtained in the topTables output for Differential Transcript Expression tests, stratified by DE label. P-values are obtained using a two-tailed t.test with respect to the non DE group (ns: p > 0.05, *: p <= 0.05, **: p <= 0.01, ***: p <= 0.001, and ****: p <= 0.0001).

text:
Focous only on direct RNA vs PCR cDNA comparisson below
GC content and sequence legnth calculated for all transcripts annotated in ‘bambu’ extended_annotation.gtf file, using bedtools nuc (sequence length is the sum of all bases and GC content is the ratio of sums of G’s and C’s to the total length). Values presented for all transcripts tested, stratified by DE label. P-values are obtained using a two-tailed t.test with respect to the non DE group )ns: p > 0.05, *: p <= 0.05, **: p <= 0.01, ***: p <= 0.001, and ****: p <= 0.0001).

text:
2.4 Figure 2f RNA modification
m6A detection results are available from the SGNex database for a subset of the direct RNA samples in the core dataset (A549, K562, Hct116, HepG2, MCF7). For each of the cell lines, m6A site modification probability was calculated using m6Anet infer based on the 2-3 replicates that were provided for each of the cell lines. For each isoform, we first counted the number of sites that were methylated with probability >0.9 (the threshold for true modification suggested by the authors), and then calculated the fraction of these sites out of all possible methylation sites on that isoform, to provide a single value that represents methylation fraction for each isoform.

facet_grid(cols = vars(cell_line), rows = vars(assay)) +#factor(contrast , levels = contrasts_order)), cols = vars(cell_line))

title: 4 Differential Transcript Usage between protocols  (Figure 3a & Supp. Figure 3)
text:
Testing for significant DTU between the PCR cDNA and direct RNA samples was performed separately for each cell line using DRIMSeq v.1.34.0 (41), based on all transcript counts. False discovery rate (FDR) was controlled using a Two-stage test implemented in stageR v.1.28.0 (42), as recommended by the authors in (43).
After testing for DTU, we use the significance labels together with the estimated proportions for each group to identify the following cases, and define reference and test transcripts that will be compared to each other in later stages of the analysis

Code
all_cell_lines_summarised_results <- map_dfr(results, ~.x$summaraise_dmsq_stageR_results, .id = "source")


unique_gene_DTU_labels <- all_cell_lines_summarised_results%>%dplyr::select(source,geneID,gene,gene_05label) %>% distinct()




concordance_between_celllines <- unique_gene_DTU_labels%>%group_by(geneID,gene_05label) %>%mutate(dtu_cellline_num = sum(abs(gene_05label)))%>%ungroup()


concordance_between_celllines$collapsed_dtu_celline_num <- fct_collapse(
factor(concordance_between_celllines$dtu_cellline_num),"0" =c(0),"1" = c(1),"2-4" = c(2,3,4),"5-7"=c(5,6,7))


dtu_gene_concordance_plot <- concordance_between_celllines %>%filter(gene_05label !=0) %>%group_by(source,collapsed_dtu_celline_num)%>%summarise(sum = n())%>%
  ggplot(aes(x =source,y=sum,fill = factor(collapsed_dtu_celline_num ))) + geom_bar(stat ="identity",position = "stack") +  
  geom_text(aes(label = sum), position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5), size = 3) +
  labs(y="Genes",x="",fill="Concordance between cell lines") + 
  #facet_grid(cols = vars(factor(contrast, levels = contrasts_order)),scales ="free") + 
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 8),legend.position = "bottom") + scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Oranges") # 



ragg::agg_png("figures/sup_figure3/dtu_gene_concordance.png", width = 6, height = 5, units = "cm", res = 600, scaling = .5)
plot(dtu_gene_concordance_plot )
invisible(dev.off())


add_TestedTotal <- left_join( concordance_between_celllines %>%filter(gene_05label !=0) %>%group_by(source,collapsed_dtu_celline_num)%>%summarise(sum = n())%>%ungroup(),
  concordance_between_celllines%>%group_by(source)%>%summarise(total_tested = n()) %>%ungroup(), by = join_by(source)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x =source,y=sum,fill = factor(collapsed_dtu_celline_num ))) + geom_bar(stat ="identity",position = "stack") +  
  geom_text(aes(label = sum), position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5), size = 2) +
  labs(y="Genes",x="",fill="Number of cell-lines") + 
  #facet_grid(cols = vars(factor(contrast, levels = contrasts_order)),scales ="free") + 
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size = 6,angle = 30),legend.position = "bottom",
        axis.title.y.right = element_text(color = "darkblue") ) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Oranges") + 
  #coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0,1500)) +
  geom_point(aes(y=total_tested/3),shape = 4,color="darkblue") +
  scale_y_continuous(
    name = "Genes",
    # Add a second axis and specify its features

section: 3.1 Add sequence length and GC content
text:
3.1 Add sequence length and GC content


Code
nuc_data_filename <- "allTXexon_sums_ont_no_ev_counts.csv"

TX_info <- read.csv(paste(output_path,"extra_info/",nuc_data_filename,sep=""))

TX_info <- TX_info %>% mutate(log2seqLen = log2(allNUC))


relevant_TX_info <- TX_info %>%dplyr::select(c( "transcript_id", "exon_sumGCcontent","allNUC","log2seqLen"))





merged_list <- lapply(results, function(source) {
  lapply(names(source), function(tibble_name) {
    tibble <- source[[tibble_name]]
    
    # Process tibbles that start with specific prefix
    if (tibble_name=="background_pairs") {
  
      
      tibble %>%left_join(relevant_TX_info,
        by = join_by("txID.plusGroupTopTx" == "transcript_id"),suffix = c("left","right"))%>%left_join(TX_info %>%dplyr::select(c( "transcript_id", "exon_sumGCcontent","allNUC","log2seqLen")),#, "exon_sumSeqLen")),
        by = join_by("txID.plusGroup2ndTx" == "transcript_id"),suffix = c("_ref","_test")) %>% mutate(GC_diff = exon_sumGCcontent_ref-exon_sumGCcontent_test, length_diff = allNUC_ref-allNUC_test)%>% mutate(log2_length_diff = log2(length_diff)) %>%
         dplyr::select(c(geneID,mean_expression.x, GC_diff, length_diff, log2_length_diff))
    }
    else if
        ( tibble_name=="switching_pairs") {
   
          
      tibble %>%left_join(relevant_TX_info,
        by = join_by("txID.plusGroupHigh" == "transcript_id"),suffix = c("left","right"))%>%left_join(TX_info %>%dplyr::select(c( "transcript_id", "exon_sumGCcontent","allNUC","log2seqLen")),#, "exon_sumSeqLen")),
        by = join_by("txID.minusGroupHigh" == "transcript_id"),suffix = c("_ref","_test")) %>% mutate(GC_diff = exon_sumGCcontent_ref-exon_sumGCcontent_test, length_diff = allNUC_ref-allNUC_test)%>% mutate(log2_length_diff = log2(length_diff))%>%
         dplyr::select(c(geneID,mean_expression.x, GC_diff, length_diff, log2_length_diff))
   
    
        }
  }) %>%
  setNames(names(source))
})

    sup3_bottom, ncol = 1, nrows = 2, heights = c(1, 4))

    ""), ncol = 2, nrow = 1, legend = "bottom", common.legend = TRUE)

# %>%annotate_figure(top = 'Number of DTU features detected between direct RNA
# and PCR cDNA samples')


fig3_top_panel %>%
    ggexport(filename = "figures/figure3/fig3_top_panel.pdf", width = 15, height = 5)





Supplementary Figure 3: a) Gene level Mean expression separated by background or dominant switching gene. b) Difference in GC content between reference and test transcripts calculated for each gene, separated to background and dominant switching. c) Difference in sequence length between reference and test, in background and dominant switching genes. Recall in both cases, the transcript most dominant in dRNA is considered ‘reference’. In genes with dominant switching, the transcript dominant in the PCRcDNA is considered ‘test’, and in background genes the second most dominant transcript is considered ‘test’. d) Sign[diffLength] for background and switching transcript pairs. +/-1 (blue/red) indicate that the transcript most dominant in direct RNA is longer/shorter than the transcript dominant in cDNA for switching. transcript pairs (second most dominant in the background transcript pairs). In around 60% of the background transcript pairs the transcript dominant in the direct RNA is longer. While in the switching transcript pairs it is closer to 50% for all cell types. P-values were obtained using a two-tailed t.test, with respect to the background group.

title: 4 Differential Transcript Usage between protocols  (Figure 3a & Supp. Figure 3)
text:
Footnotes


quote and relevant code snippet from https://bioconductor.org/packages/release/bioc/vignettes/DRIMSeq/inst/doc/DRIMSeq.pdf Dated April 30, 2024 Package: DRIMSeq 1.32.0 Two-stage test DRIMSeq returns gene and transcript level p-values which can be used as an input to the stage-wise analysis [2] implemented in the stageR package, currently available on github https://github.com/statOmics/stageR. As pointed by the authors of stageR, interpreting both gene-level and transcript-level adjusted p-values does not provide appropriate FDR control and should be avoided. However, applying a stage-wise testing provides a useful biological interpretation of these results and improved statistical performance. In short, the procedure consists of a screening stage and a confirmation stage. In the screening stage, gene-level BH-adjusted p-values are screened to detect genes for which the hypothesis of interest is significantly rejected. Only those genes are further considered in the confirmation stage, where for each gene separately, transcript-level p-values are adjusted to control the FWER and BH-adjusted significance level of the screening stage. It is important to note that transcript-level stage-wise adjusted p-values for genes that do not pass the screening stage are set to NA. Also the stage-wise adjusted p-values can not be compared to significance level other than chosen in the stage-wise analysis. If that is of interest, one has to rerun this analysis with the new significance level. The following code chunk is not evaluated by this vignette and to run it, user has to make sure that the stageR package is installed. It shows how one can use the DRIMSeq output in the stage-wise analysis.↩︎

title: 5 Examples of Structural differences detected in A549 (Figure 3b-d)
text:
Visualise structural differences found between ref and test transcripts for a few example genes detected as DTU in the A549 cell-line. For completeness, coverage plots are included for each region of interest (based on sample .bam files aligned to genome downloaded from the SGN-ex database). Transcript CPM obtained with bambu, and transcript proportions estimated with DRIMSeq are also presented.
This file includes examples for:
crumbs: 5 Examples of  Structural differences detected in A549 (Figure 3b-d)

title: 5 Examples of Structural differences detected in A549 (Figure 3b-d)
text:
Setup, imports and parameters


Code
import sys
sys.path.append("/Users/rotem/Code/manuscript-and-analysis-code/src/python")




Code
import os
import matplotlib
from math import nan
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import transparent
import plotnine as p9
import logging




Code
# Example for a single contrast
cell_line = "A549"
assay = "counts"
contrast_name = "dRNAvsPCRcDNA"
case_name = cell_line + assay + contrast_name


project_path = "/Users/rotem/Code/manuscript-and-analysis-code/"
data_path = "../../data/"
output_path = "../../data/analysis_output/ont_only_counts/"
figures_path = "figures/"


output_prefix = "data/analysis_output/ont_only_counts/"
figures_prefix = "figures/"
publication_figures = "manuscript/figures/"

bambu_ann_filename = "bambu_output/ont_only_counts/extended_annotations.gtf"
ann_path = data_path + bambu_ann_filename  # "bambu/extended_annotations.gtf"
cpm = "bambu_output/ont_only_counts/CPM_transcript.txt"
cpm_file_path = data_path + cpm  # "/Users/aharonrotem/Projects/LongReads/data/


Based on all exon start and end positions on the genome, all unmatched positions were identified and marked on the test transcript with respect to the reference transcript. This identification step accommodates for small variations in transcript annotation that could be a result of an inaccurate identification of the splice junction, by allowing for unmatched positions that are in very close proximity (here bp=20 ) to be considered as matched. Unmatched positions were characterised and labelled based on their relationship to the surrounding features, for example: ‘exon skipping’, ‘intron insertion’, etc. This can result in multiple events allocated to any specific comparison between transcripts. When these events occur consecutively, without any matched junctions between them, they are considered linked events. For example, consecutive skipped exons are labeled ‘linked deletion’, and consecutive inserted exons are considered ‘linked insertion’, as it is likely they are the result of a single splicing event. An alternative exon, which in our case is identified as an exon skipping event followed by an exon insertion, or vice versa, is labeled a ‘linked combination’ event (similar to an ‘exon extension’ event followed by an ‘exon skipping’, or many other possible combinations). Additional details regarding events detected in the test compared with the reference are also collected, such as their position, length, and a label to indicate event’s position on the transcript (3’ , 5’, or internal). We also quantify and visualise the structural differences detected, along with other features which enable integration with coverage data (e.g. for qualitative validation), annotation level information (such as CDS and ORF), and more.

title: 8 Examples of dominant switching and novel isoform comparisons in Hct116
text:
Demonstrate differences found between ref and test transcripts for a few example genes, detected as DTU in the Hct116 cell-line. For each gene, transcript CPM obtained with bambu, proportions estimated by DRIMSeq, and structural differences obtained with transParent are presented. For completeness, coverage plots are also provided (based on sample .bam files aligned to genome from the SGN-ex database).
This file includes examples of:

cpm = "bambu_output/ont_only_counts/CPM_transcript.txt"
cpm_file_path = data_path + cpm  # "/Users/aharonrotem/Projects/LongReads/data/


Based on all exon start and end positions on the genome, all unmatched positions were identified and marked on the test transcript with respect to the reference transcript. This identification step accommodates for small variations in transcript annotation that could be a result of an inaccurate identification of the splice junction, by allowing for unmatched positions that are in very close proximity (here bp=20 ) to be considered as matched. Unmatched positions were characterised and labelled based on their relationship to the surrounding features, for example: ‘exon skipping’, ‘intron insertion’, etc. This can result in multiple events allocated to any specific comparison between transcripts. When these events occur consecutively, without any matched junctions between them, they are considered linked events. For example, consecutive skipped exons are labeled ‘linked deletion’, and consecutive inserted exons are considered ‘linked insertion’, as it is likely they are the result of a single splicing event. An alternative exon, which in our case is identified as an exon skipping event followed by an exon insertion, or vice versa, is labeled a ‘linked combination’ event (similar to an ‘exon extension’ event followed by an ‘exon skipping’, or many other possible combinations). Additional details regarding events detected in the test compared with the reference are also collected, such as their position, length, and a label to indicate event’s position on the transcript (3’ , 5’, or internal). We also quantify and visualise the structural differences detected, along with other features which enable integration with coverage data (e.g. for qualitative validation), annotation level information (such as CDS and ORF), and more.

all_dtu_pairs = collect_results[(
    collect_results['pair_label'] == 'Switching isoforms')]

all_dtu_pairs.loc[:, "sample_id"] = "switching_" + \
    all_dtu_pairs.loc[:, "cell_line"]

# all_dtu_pairs_test = collect_results[(
#     collect_results['pair_label'] == 'test_ Switching isoforms')]

# all_dtu_pairs_test.loc[:, "sample_id"] = "switching_test_" + \
#     all_dtu_pairs_test.loc[:, "cell_line"]


results_to_plot = pd.concat([all_dtu_pairs])  # ,all_dtu_pairs_test])


# %%
results_to_plot.loc[:, "sample"] = results_to_plot.loc[:,
                                                       "sample_id"].str.split("_", expand=True)[1]
explode_linked_events = results_to_plot.explode("linked_events_labels")
explode_linked_events.linked_events_labels
explode_linked_events["truncated_linked_event_name"] = explode_linked_events.linked_events_labels.str.split(
    "_", expand=True)[0]
explode_linked_events["truncated_linked_edge_label"] = explode_linked_events.linked_events_labels.str.split(
    "_", expand=True)[1]


# remove all cases of "no_events" labels (needed to account for background pairs in which the transcripts passes are actually identical despite having different id's)
explode_linked_events = explode_linked_events[explode_linked_events["truncated_linked_edge_label"] .isin([
                                                                                                         '5prime', 'internal', '3prime'])]

3.4 Sample sets of background genes to match meanExp distribution


Code
from transparent._utilities import get_bin_stats, sample_groups_varying_n




Code
upper_lim = np.log2(5000)

set_bins = np.arange(0, upper_lim+25, 1)


upper_lim = np.log2(5000)
lower_lim = np.log2(0+1)
n_bins = 50
collect_results["binned_expression"] = np.log2(
    collect_results.loc[:, "mean_expression.x"])
collect_results['mean_expression'] = np.log2(
    collect_results.loc[:, "mean_expression.x"])


# complete these cases to capture outliers

collect_results["binned_expression"] = np.where(
    collect_results['mean_expression'] < lower_lim, lower_lim, collect_results['mean_expression'])
collect_results["binned_expression"] = np.where(
    collect_results['mean_expression'] > upper_lim, upper_lim, collect_results['mean_expression'])




Code
# Sample from background pairs to match dtu pairs mean expression distribution
all_background_pairs = collect_results[collect_results['pair_label']
                                       == 'Background isoforms']
all_dtu_pairs = collect_results[collect_results['pair_label']
                                == 'Switching isoforms']

dtu_data, weights_data = get_bin_stats(
    all_dtu_pairs, "binned_expression", n_bins=set_bins)
dtu_weights_dict = dict(zip(weights_data['left'], weights_data["proportions"]))

dtu_data = pd.merge(dtu_data, weights_data, left_on="bins",
                    right_on="bin", how="inner")


background_data, weights_data = get_bin_stats(
    all_background_pairs, "binned_expression", n_bins=set_bins)
background_weights_dict = dict(
    zip(weights_data['left'], weights_data["proportions"]))

background_data = pd.merge(
    background_data, weights_data, left_on="bins", right_on="bin", how="inner")


collect_samples = pd.DataFrame()
for i in np.arange(5):
    sampled_df = sample_groups_varying_n(
        df=background_data, group_col="left", overall_samples=100, weights_dict=dtu_weights_dict)
    sampled_df.loc[:, "sample_id"] = "sample_" + str(i)
    collect_samples = pd.concat([collect_samples, sampled_df])

    sampled_df.mean_expression.hist(bins=100)
    # plt.xlim(0,2000)

# Sample from background pairs to match dtu pairs mean expression distribution
collect_samples = pd.DataFrame()
for cell_line in ["A549", "H9", "MCF7", "HEYA8", "HepG2", "K562", "Hct116"]:
    all_background_pairs = collect_results[(collect_results['pair_label'] == 'Background isoforms') & (
        collect_results['cell_line'] == cell_line)]
    all_dtu_pairs = collect_results[(collect_results['pair_label'] == 'Switching isoforms') & (
        collect_results['cell_line'] == cell_line)]

    dtu_data, weights_data = get_bin_stats(
        all_dtu_pairs, "binned_expression", n_bins=set_bins)
    dtu_weights_dict = dict(
        zip(weights_data['left'], weights_data["proportions"]))

    dtu_data = pd.merge(dtu_data, weights_data,
                        left_on="bins", right_on="bin", how="inner")

    background_data, weights_data = get_bin_stats(
        all_background_pairs, "binned_expression", n_bins=set_bins)
    background_weights_dict = dict(
        zip(weights_data['left'], weights_data["proportions"]))

    background_data = pd.merge(
        background_data, weights_data, left_on="bins", right_on="bin", how="inner")

    # collect_samples = pd.DataFrame()
    for i in np.arange(5):
        sampled_df = sample_groups_varying_n(
            df=background_data, group_col="left", overall_samples=100, weights_dict=dtu_weights_dict)
        sampled_df.loc[:, "sample_id"] = "sample_" + str(i) + "_"+cell_line
        collect_samples = pd.concat([collect_samples, sampled_df])

        sampled_df.mean_expression.hist(bins=100)
        plt.xlim(0, 2000)

all_dtu_pairs = collect_results[(
    collect_results['pair_label'] == 'Switching isoforms')]

all_dtu_pairs.loc[:, "sample_id"] = "switching_" + \
    all_dtu_pairs.loc[:, "cell_line"]

# all_dtu_pairs_test = collect_results[(
#     collect_results['pair_label'] == 'test_ Switching isoforms')]

# all_dtu_pairs_test.loc[:, "sample_id"] = "switching_test_" + \
#     all_dtu_pairs_test.loc[:, "cell_line"]


results_to_plot = pd.concat([all_dtu_pairs])  # ,all_dtu_pairs_test])


# %%
results_to_plot.loc[:, "sample"] = results_to_plot.loc[:,
                                                       "sample_id"].str.split("_", expand=True)[1]
explode_linked_events = results_to_plot.explode("linked_events_labels")
explode_linked_events.linked_events_labels
explode_linked_events["truncated_linked_event_name"] = explode_linked_events.linked_events_labels.str.split(
    "_", expand=True)[0]
explode_linked_events["truncated_linked_edge_label"] = explode_linked_events.linked_events_labels.str.split(
    "_", expand=True)[1]


# remove all cases of "no_events" labels (needed to account for background pairs in which the transcripts passes are actually identical despite having different id's)
explode_linked_events = explode_linked_events[explode_linked_events["truncated_linked_edge_label"] .isin([
                                                                                                         '5prime', 'internal', '3prime'])]

).reset_index(), on=["cell_line", "pair_label", "truncated_linked_edge_label"], how="left")
prop_df["group_proportion"] = prop_df.feature_iddRNA_x / \

3.4 Sample sets of background genes to match meanExp distribution


Code
from pkg_name.utilities import get_bin_stats, sample_groups_varying_n




Code
upper_lim = np.log2(5000)

set_bins = np.arange(0, upper_lim+25, 1)


upper_lim = np.log2(5000)
lower_lim = np.log2(0+1)
n_bins = 50
collect_results["binned_expression"] = np.log2(
    collect_results.loc[:, "mean_expression.x"])
collect_results['mean_expression'] = np.log2(
    collect_results.loc[:, "mean_expression.x"])


# complete these cases to capture outliers

collect_results["binned_expression"] = np.where(
    collect_results['mean_expression'] < lower_lim, lower_lim, collect_results['mean_expression'])
collect_results["binned_expression"] = np.where(
    collect_results['mean_expression'] > upper_lim, upper_lim, collect_results['mean_expression'])




Code
# Sample from background pairs to match dtu pairs mean expression distribution
all_background_pairs = collect_results[collect_results['pair_label']
                                       == 'Background isoforms']
all_dtu_pairs = collect_results[collect_results['pair_label']
                                == 'Switching isoforms']

dtu_data, weights_data = get_bin_stats(
    all_dtu_pairs, "binned_expression", n_bins=set_bins)
dtu_weights_dict = dict(zip(weights_data['left'], weights_data["proportions"]))

dtu_data = pd.merge(dtu_data, weights_data, left_on="bins",
                    right_on="bin", how="inner")


background_data, weights_data = get_bin_stats(
    all_background_pairs, "binned_expression", n_bins=set_bins)
background_weights_dict = dict(
    zip(weights_data['left'], weights_data["proportions"]))

background_data = pd.merge(
    background_data, weights_data, left_on="bins", right_on="bin", how="inner")


collect_samples = pd.DataFrame()
for i in np.arange(5):
    sampled_df = sample_groups_varying_n(
        df=background_data, group_col="left", overall_samples=100, weights_dict=dtu_weights_dict)
    sampled_df.loc[:, "sample_id"] = "sample_" + str(i)
    collect_samples = pd.concat([collect_samples, sampled_df])

    sampled_df.mean_expression.hist(bins=100)
    # plt.xlim(0,2000)

# Sample from background pairs to match dtu pairs mean expression distribution
collect_samples = pd.DataFrame()
for cell_line in ["A549", "H9", "MCF7", "HEYA8", "HepG2", "K562", "Hct116"]:
    all_background_pairs = collect_results[(collect_results['pair_label'] == 'Background isoforms') & (
        collect_results['cell_line'] == cell_line)]
    all_dtu_pairs = collect_results[(collect_results['pair_label'] == 'Switching isoforms') & (
        collect_results['cell_line'] == cell_line)]

    dtu_data, weights_data = get_bin_stats(
        all_dtu_pairs, "binned_expression", n_bins=set_bins)
    dtu_weights_dict = dict(
        zip(weights_data['left'], weights_data["proportions"]))

    dtu_data = pd.merge(dtu_data, weights_data,
                        left_on="bins", right_on="bin", how="inner")

    background_data, weights_data = get_bin_stats(
        all_background_pairs, "binned_expression", n_bins=set_bins)
    background_weights_dict = dict(
        zip(weights_data['left'], weights_data["proportions"]))

    background_data = pd.merge(
        background_data, weights_data, left_on="bins", right_on="bin", how="inner")

    # collect_samples = pd.DataFrame()
    for i in np.arange(5):
        sampled_df = sample_groups_varying_n(
            df=background_data, group_col="left", overall_samples=100, weights_dict=dtu_weights_dict)
        sampled_df.loc[:, "sample_id"] = "sample_" + str(i) + "_"+cell_line
        collect_samples = pd.concat([collect_samples, sampled_df])

        sampled_df.mean_expression.hist(bins=100)
        plt.xlim(0, 2000)

                                                       "sample_id"].str.split("_", expand=True)[1]

explode_linked_events.linked_events_labels

    "_", expand=True)[0]
explode_linked_events["truncated_linked_edge_label"] = explode_linked_events.linked_events_labels.str.split(
    "_", expand=True)[1]


# remove all cases of "no_events" labels (needed to account for background pairs in which the transcripts passes are actually identical despite having different id's)
explode_linked_events = explode_linked_events[explode_linked_events["truncated_linked_edge_label"] .isin([
                                                                                                         '5prime', 'internal', '3prime'])]

Code
from transparent._utilities import get_bin_stats, sample_groups_varying_n




Code
upper_lim = np.log2(5000)

set_bins = np.arange(0, upper_lim+25, 1)


upper_lim = np.log2(5000)
lower_lim = np.log2(0+1)
n_bins = 50
collect_results["binned_expression"] = np.log2(
    collect_results.loc[:, "mean_expression.x"])
collect_results['mean_expression'] = np.log2(
    collect_results.loc[:, "mean_expression.x"])


# complete these cases to capture outliers

collect_results["binned_expression"] = np.where(
    collect_results['mean_expression'] < lower_lim, lower_lim, collect_results['mean_expression'])
collect_results["binned_expression"] = np.where(
    collect_results['mean_expression'] > upper_lim, upper_lim, collect_results['mean_expression'])

# 1. Sort the unique values alphabetically
sorted_categories = ["A549",  "H9", "Hct116",
                     "HepG2", "HEYA8",  "K562", "MCF7"]

# 2. Assign the sorted categorical dtype back to the column
prop_df['sorted_cell_line'] = pd.Categorical(
    prop_df['cell_line'],
    categories=sorted_categories,
    ordered=True
)


fig3g = p9.ggplot(data=prop_df, mapping=p9.aes(x='sorted_cell_line', y="feature_iddRNA_x", fill="ordered_events")) + p9.geom_bar(stat="identity") + p9.facet_grid(cols="truncated_linked_edge_label",
                                                                                                                                                                  scales="free") + p9.labs(x="", y="", fill="") + p9.theme(axis_text_x=p9.element_text(size=8)) + p9.scale_fill_manual(values=DIFF_COLORS_HEX, limits=ORDER_EVENTS)
fig_size = [12, 4]
